name: Izhar
definition: To pronounce the noon sakinah (نْ) or tanween (ً ٍ ٌ) clearly and distinctly, without any nasal sound (ghunnah), when followed by one of the six throat letters.
urdu_def: نون ساکن یا تنوین کو گلے کے چھ حروف سے پہلے بالکل واضح اور صاف پڑھنا، کسی غنہ کے بغیر۔
mnemonic: أخي هاع — أَخِي هَاعَ (These 6 letters come from your throat)
how_to: Read نْ or tanween in your nasal passage normally
When you see one of the 6 throat letters after it
Pronounce the نْ/tanween CLEARLY — full sound
No nasal resonance — cut it clean
Move straight into the throat letter

examples.note: نْ before ء — say it clearly

tip: Think of it as a 'full stop' — the noon/tanween sound ends completely before the throat letter starts.
common_mistake: Adding ghunnah (nasal hum) to the noon — don't prolong the nasal sound.

name: Idgham
definition: To merge the noon sakinah (نْ) or tanween into the following letter so that only one merged letter remains. It occurs before 6 specific letters. Divided into two types: with ghunnah (nasal) and without ghunnah.
urdu_def: نون ساکن یا تنوین کو اگلے حرف میں ملا دینا تاکہ صرف ایک مشدد حرف رہ جائے۔
mnemonic: يَرْمَلُون — Remember this word: its letters are the 6 Idgham letters

subtypes.name: Idgham with Ghunnah
subtypes.desc: Merge the noon into these 4 letters AND hold a nasal (ghunnah) for 2 counts

subtypes.name: Idgham without Ghunnah
subtypes.desc: Merge the noon into ل or ر completely — no nasal sound at all

how_to: See نْ or tanween followed by one of the 6 letters
Do NOT pronounce the نْ at all — skip it
Go directly into the next letter
If it's ي ن م و — add 2 counts of nasal hum (ghunnah)
If it's ل ر — merge and continue cleanly, no nasal
tip: Idgham only applies when نْ and the following letter are in TWO DIFFERENT WORDS. If in the same word (like نَّ), it becomes a shaddah.
common_mistake: Saying نْ fully before merging. The noon should disappear completely.

name: Iqlab
short: Change noon/tanween to meem before ب
definition: To change (substitute) the noon sakinah or tanween into a meem sound, when followed by the letter ب (ba), and hold the nasal (ghunnah) for 2 counts.
urdu_def: نون ساکن یا تنوین کو 'ب' سے پہلے میم کی آواز میں بدل دینا اور دو حرکات غنہ کے ساتھ ادا کرنا۔
mnemonic: Only ONE letter triggers Iqlab — and that's ب. Easy to remember!
how_to: See نْ or tanween followed by ب
Do NOT say نْ — convert it to a MEEM sound
Close your lips slightly (like meem)
Hold the nasal ghunnah for 2 counts
Then open lips and pronounce the ب
tip: In written Qurans, an iqlab is marked with a small م above the noon or tanween, helping you recognize it instantly.
common_mistake: Pronouncing the نْ fully before ب — you should not hear any 'n' sound. Only the meem (lips together) + ghunnah.

name: Ikhfa
short: Hide noon/tanween — between clear and merged
definition: To pronounce the noon sakinah or tanween in a state between Izhar (clear) and Idgham (merged) — with ghunnah for 2 counts — when followed by 15 specific letters.
urdu_def: نون ساکن یا تنوین کو پندرہ حروف سے پہلے اظہار اور ادغام کے درمیان کی حالت میں پڑھنا، دو حرکات غنہ کے ساتھ۔
mnemonic: All letters EXCEPT the 6 throat letters (Izhar), 6 yarmaloon letters (Idgham), and ب (Iqlab) = Ikhfa!
how_to: See نْ or tanween before one of the 15 letters
Do NOT pronounce نْ fully (no Izhar)
Do NOT merge it completely (no Idgham)
Hold ghunnah for 2 counts with tongue/lips positioned for the NEXT letter
Then proceed to the next letter

examples.note: نْ before ت — hide with ghunnah

tip: Your tongue/lips should be positioned for the next letter WHILE you hum the ghunnah — a preparation + nasal hum together.
common_mistake: Either reading نْ too clearly (becomes Izhar) or merging completely (becomes Idgham). Aim for the middle.

name: Ghunnah
definition: A nasal sound that resonates from the nose for 2 full counts. It is ALWAYS applied on noon (ن) or meem (م) that carries a shaddah (ّ).
urdu_def: ناک سے نکلنے والی آواز جو نون مشدد یا میم مشدد پر لازمی ہوتی ہے — دو حرکات کے لیے۔
mnemonic: Shaddah on ن or م = ALWAYS 2 counts of ghunnah. No exceptions!
how_to: See نّ or مّ (with shaddah)
Place tongue/lips for that letter
Hum from your NOSE — not throat
Hold the nasal hum for exactly 2 counts
Then release into the next letter
tip: Block your nose with your fingers while reading — if the sound stops, you're doing ghunnah correctly from your nose!
common_mistake: Making ghunnah too short (less than 2 counts) or applying it on regular ن and م without shaddah.

name: Qalqalah
short: Echoing bounce on 5 letters with sukoon
definition: A slight echoing or bouncing vibration in the sound produced when one of the 5 Qalqalah letters has a sukoon (ْ) — either naturally or at a pause.
urdu_def: پانچ مخصوص حروف پر جب سکون ہو تو انہیں ایک خاص ارتعاش کے ساتھ ادا کرنا۔
mnemonic: قُطْبُ جَدٍّ — These two words contain ALL 5 Qalqalah letters!

subtypes.name: Qalqalah Sughra (Minor)
subtypes.desc: When the qalqalah letter has sukoon in the MIDDLE of a word — slight echo

subtypes.name: Qalqalah Kubra (Major)
subtypes.desc: When you PAUSE on a qalqalah letter at the END of a word/ayah — stronger, more prominent echo

how_to: Identify one of the 5 letters: ق ط ب ج د
Check if it has sukoon (ْ) or if you're pausing on it
Begin pronouncing the letter from its makhraj
Before releasing, create a slight 'echo' or 'bounce'
For end of ayah (Kubra): the echo is stronger and longer

examples.note: لْ after ق — pause: Kubra on ل

tip: Surah Al-Ikhlas (قُلْ هُوَ اللَّهُ أَحَدٌ) is the BEST practice — it has multiple qalqalah letters at the end of every ayah!
common_mistake: Not bouncing at all, or over-exaggerating into a full 'ha' sound. It should be subtle but distinct.

name: Madd Tabee
definition: The natural (original) elongation that occurs on one of the three madd letters — alif (ا), ya (ي), waw (و) — when they are preceded by a letter with their corresponding vowel. It is held for exactly 2 counts.
urdu_def: تین حروف مد (الف، واو، یا) پر دو حرکات کی لمبی آواز — یہ سب سے بنیادی مد ہے۔
trigger_label: 3 Madd Letters — ا و ي (Alif, Waw, Ya)
mnemonic: Alif after fatha | Waw after damma | Ya after kasra = 2 counts. Simple and natural!
how_to: See ا preceded by a fatha (ـَا)
OR see و preceded by a damma (ـُو)
OR see ي preceded by a kasra (ـِي)
Elongate that vowel sound for exactly 2 counts
Do not cut it short or overextend
tip: Count '1-2' in your head while elongating. If no vowel precedes the madd letter, or another madd rule applies — it becomes a different type of madd.
common_mistake: Cutting the madd too short (less than 2 counts) or not elongating at all.

name: Madd Muttasil
short: Connected madd — madd letter + hamza IN same word
definition: When a madd letter (ا و ي) is immediately followed by a hamza (ء) within THE SAME WORD — elongate for 4 to 5 counts. This is obligatory (wajib).
urdu_def: ایک ہی کلمے میں حرف مد کے بعد ہمزہ آئے تو 4 یا 5 حرکات تک کھینچنا لازم ہے۔
trigger_label: Madd letter + Hamza (ء) — in SAME word
mnemonic: Muttasil = Connected — the madd and hamza are CONNECTED in one word
how_to: Find a madd letter (ا و ي) inside a word
Check: is the VERY NEXT letter a hamza (ء)?
Is it all in ONE word? → Muttasil
Elongate for 4 counts minimum (some reciters: 5)
Stay consistent throughout your recitation

examples.note: ا then ء in same word — 4-5 counts

tip: In the Quran, Muttasil is often marked with a special symbol (ۤ or a curved madd sign). Look for it!
common_mistake: Reading it like a Tabee (only 2 counts). Muttasil MUST be 4+ counts — it's obligatory.

name: Madd Munfasil
short: Separated madd — madd letter then hamza in NEXT word
definition: When a word ends with a madd letter and the NEXT word begins with a hamza (ء). Unlike Muttasil, this is in TWO SEPARATE words. Elongation is 4–5 counts (or 2 counts in the Qasr method).
urdu_def: ایک لفظ حرف مد پر ختم ہو اور اگلا لفظ ہمزہ سے شروع ہو — دونوں الگ الگ کلمے۔
trigger_label: Madd letter at end of word + Hamza (ء) starting NEXT word
mnemonic: Munfasil = Separated — the madd and hamza are in SEPARATE words
how_to: A word ends with ا / و / ي (madd letter)
The next word starts with ء (hamza)
They are in TWO different words
Elongate 4–5 counts (Hafs 'an Asim method)
You may also use 2 counts (called Qasr)

examples.note: نَا ends, then أَ begins next word
ي madd, then أَ next word

tip: When in doubt: is the hamza in the SAME word or NEXT word? Same = Muttasil (4-5). Next = Munfasil (4-5 or 2).
common_mistake: Confusing Munfasil with Muttasil. Check if it's one word or two!

name: Madd Arid Lissukoon
short: Madd at pause — last letter gets sukoon when stopping
definition: When you pause (waqf) at the end of an ayah or word, and the last syllable contains a madd letter — the pause causes a temporary sukoon, extending the elongation. You may elongate for 2, 4, or 6 counts.
urdu_def: جب کسی آیت یا لفظ کے آخر پر وقف کریں اور آخری حرف پر مد آئے تو اسے 2، 4 یا 6 حرکات تک لمبا کرنا جائز ہے۔
trigger_label: Pause (Waqf) at end of ayah/word with madd letter before last letter
mnemonic: Arid = Occasional/Temporary. The sukoon happens ONLY because you paused — it's not written in the text.
how_to: You reach the end of an ayah or decide to pause
The last letter will get a sukoon when you pause
If a madd letter comes just before the last letter
Elongate: 2 counts (minimum), 4 counts (medium), 6 counts (maximum)
All three are valid — choose one and stay consistent
tip: Al-Fatiha is PERFECT for practicing Madd Arid — every ayah ending has one!
common_mistake: Continuing with Madd Arid while reading without stopping. If you don't pause, it becomes a Madd Tabee (2 counts only).

name: Lam Shamsia
short: Al- lam disappears — absorbed into next letter
definition: When the definite article اَلـ (al-) is followed by one of 14 solar (shams) letters, the lam is NOT pronounced. Instead it merges into the following letter, which is then doubled (shaddah).
urdu_def: جب اَلـ کے بعد چودہ شمسی حروف میں سے کوئی آئے تو لام کو ادا نہیں کیا جاتا — اگلا حرف مشدد ہوجاتا ہے۔
mnemonic: طِبْ ثُمَّ صِلْ رَحِمًا تَفُزْ ضِفْ ذَا نِعَمٍ دَعْ سُوءَ ظَنٍّ زُرْ شَرِيفًا لِلْكَرَمِ
how_to: See اَلـ before a word
Look at the FIRST letter of the word
Is it one of the 14 solar letters?
If YES — skip the lam, double the solar letter
Pronounce as if the lam was never there
tip: The 14 letters are the same ones that start the words in the mnemonic. Memorize the mnemonic once — you'll know all 14 forever.
common_mistake: Pronouncing اَلـ fully as 'al' before solar letters (e.g. saying 'al-shamsu' instead of 'ash-shamsu').

name: Lam Qamariya
short: Al- lam IS pronounced — clear before 14 lunar letters
definition: When the definite article اَلـ is followed by one of 14 lunar (qamar) letters, the lam IS pronounced clearly. The following letter does NOT become doubled.
urdu_def: جب اَلـ کے بعد چودہ قمری حروف میں سے کوئی آئے تو لام واضح طور پر پڑھا جاتا ہے اور اگلا حرف مشدد نہیں ہوتا۔
mnemonic: اِبْغِ حَجَّكَ وَخَفْ عَقِيمَهُ — These 14 letters = Qamariya
how_to: See اَلـ before a word
Look at the FIRST letter of the word
Is it one of the 14 lunar letters?
If YES — pronounce the لام clearly
No doubling on the next letter
tip: A sukoon (ْ) is always written on the lam of اَلـ when followed by a qamariya letter — this is your visual marker in the Quran.
common_mistake: Forgetting to pronounce the lam fully — or confusing lunar with solar letters.

name: Tafkhim
definition: Tafkhim means to pronounce a letter with a 'full' or 'heavy' sound, as if the mouth is full. There are 7 permanently heavy letters in Arabic, plus the name of Allah (اللَّه) in certain conditions.
urdu_def: کچھ حروف کو بھاری اور موٹی آواز کے ساتھ پڑھنا — ان کی ادائیگی میں منہ کی تمام جگہ استعمال ہوتی ہے۔
mnemonic: خُصَّ ضَغْطٍ قِظْ — Memorize this phrase and you know all 7 heavy letters!
how_to: Identify one of the 7 heavy letters: خ ص ض غ ط ق ظ
OR the name اللَّه when preceded by fatha or damma
Round and raise the back of your tongue
Make the sound 'full' and 'thick'
The vowel after it should also sound heavy
tip: A simple test: say 'law' vs 'lee' in English. 'Law' position = tafkhim. Heavy letters sound like the 'law' position.
common_mistake: Reading heavy letters lightly, especially ق and ط — these must be noticeably heavy.

name: Tarqiq
definition: Tarqiq means to pronounce a letter with a 'light' or 'thin' sound, without raising the back of the tongue. All Arabic letters except the 7 heavy ones are pronounced lightly (tarqiq).
urdu_def: حروف کو ہلکی اور باریک آواز کے ساتھ پڑھنا — زبان کا پچھلا حصہ اوپر نہیں اٹھتا۔
trigger_label: All letters EXCEPT the 7 heavy ones — also: Allah's name after kasra/sukoon
mnemonic: If it's NOT in خُصَّ ضَغْطٍ قِظْ → it's light (tarqiq). Simple!
how_to: Any letter that is NOT one of the 7 heavy letters
Keep the back of your tongue flat and low
Keep lips relaxed — no rounding
Sound should feel 'front' and 'forward'
Special case: اللَّه after kasra/sukoon = Tarqiq
tip: The rule of اللَّه: before a fatha or damma = heavy (tafkhim). Before a kasra or sukoon = light (tarqiq).
common_mistake: Making all Arabic letters heavy/thick. Only the 7 letters and اللَّه in certain cases are heavy.
